notes: Tests whether skill produces structured project workflows for RP projects with concrete deliverables and client-appropriate communication.

universal_assertions.text: Avoids PM jargon (stakeholders, alignment, touchpoints, deliverables-cadence)
Frames work in RP methodology terms where relevant (Bilingual Dashboard, Zero-Measurement Zone, etc.)

evals.name: new-booking-workflow
evals.prompt: I just booked Oakside Community Foundation for a Map-tier engagement ($9,000, 4-week sprint, starting Apr 14). Deliverables: Bilingual Dashboard + voice guide + 3 voice excavation sessions + final presentation to their board. Set up the project workflow: phases, milestones, client touchpoints, internal deadlines. Assume I work solo.

fixture_assertions.text: Organizes into clear phases (discovery, excavation, synthesis, delivery)
Has specific dates anchored to Apr 14 start and 4-week duration
Specifically schedules the board presentation within the timeline

evals.name: pre-shoot-prep
evals.prompt: Shoot day is Friday Apr 11 (6 days out). Client: Ama Nwosu (Bright Current founder, Mirror tier 3-session arc, this is session 2 of 3). We're filming her speaking 3 different prompts I'll give her on the day. Location: her home office. Solo operator (me + camera). Give me a prep checklist: gear, questions to prep, call-time logistics, what to send her Thursday.

fixture_assertions.text: Has a concrete gear list (camera, audio, lights)
References that prompts need to be prepped ahead of time
Specifies what to send Ama on Thursday (call time, what to wear, prep)

evals.name: phase-transition-comms
evals.prompt: Jennifer Wells (Threshold Recovery Services, Map tier) is transitioning from Phase 2 (excavation) to Phase 3 (synthesis/delivery). We just finished our 3rd excavation session. Next milestone: I deliver the Bilingual Dashboard draft by Apr 18, her review period Apr 18-25, revision cycle Apr 25-May 2, final delivery May 2. Draft the email to her that closes Phase 2 and sets up Phase 3.

fixture_assertions.text: Names something specific from the 3 excavation sessions (honors what was said)
Tone is warm (relational), not corporate project-status
